body:
MORNING INTEL — 2026-07-13 (snapshot 2026-07-13_8)
Regime data: VIX 17.32 | HY OAS 269 | Breadth 63.6%
AMZN: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
NVDA: NO_TRADE — IV Rank below minimum or missing (G2_iv_rank: observed None threshold >= 25.0)
SPY: TRADE | BULLISH conf 53.333333333333336% | Range 729.52–769.44 | put_credit_spread x1
Portfolio risk after fills: pending Artha confirmation
Discipline: 30d system PnL $0 | override PnL $0
Data quality: all sources OK

structured.takeaway: The current regime reflects elevated uncertainty with VIX at 17.32, showing near-term volatility above the 9-day average but still below the 3-month forward, indicating a mildly cautious market stance amid geopolitical tensions. SPY at $749.48 is pressured by escalating U.S.-Iran conflicts, dragging Nasdaq leaders lower and signaling near-term downside risk, while NVDA at $204.18 and AMZN at $247.21 present asymmetric opportunities; NVDA benefits from a generational tech transfer narrative with strong positive sentiment and high IV (45.68%), and AMZN’s 46.21% IV and decade-low price suggest a deep value play if geopolitical risks ease. The key catalyst to watch is this week’s inflation data and the Fed’s response, as a hot print could trigger another rate hike, compounding pressure on growth stocks and financials ahead of major bank earnings next week. The primary risk is a sustained geopolitical escalation that could spike volatility and widen credit spreads (HY OAS at 269 bps), undermining risk appetite and pushing the market into a defensive posture. Trade signal gates remain fully active, favoring selective, volatility-aware positioning.

SPY.sentiment_driver: Nasdaq leading stocks lower as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate

NVDA.sentiment_driver: NVIDIA and Micron the Winners in generational transfer
NVDA.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

AMZN.sentiment_driver: Amazon stock hasn't been this cheap in over a decade
AMZN.gate_failure_detail: IV Rank below minimum or missing

news.title: Nasdaq leading stocks lower in final hour as U.S.-Iran tensions escalate — live
Inflation to fall for first time in 6 years. Consumers won't see much relief.
A Generational Transfer Is Happening: Amazon, Google, Microsoft Are the Losers. NVIDIA and Micron the Winners.
Amazon Stock Hasn't Been This Cheap in Over a Decade. Has the Sell-Off Gone Too Far?
A hot inflation reading this week could mean a rate hike soon, Fed’s Waller says
Nasdaq and S&P 500 open lower as Iran launches new strikes
Why Citigroup is the one to watch when banks report earnings this week
Economic calendar: Treasury balance today; CPI and PPI this week
India's inflation accelerates to 4.38% in June, exceeding forecasts
The stock-market rally now hinges more on AI than oil
Oil prices rise, stock futures dip after latest flare-up of U.S.-Iran strikes

narratives.this_week: Bank earnings dominate this week with JPM, BAC, WFC, and GS reporting starting July 14, followed by MS on July 15. Investors will focus on loan growth, credit quality, and trading revenue amid a VIX at 17.32 signaling moderate risk appetite. Strong results could lift financials and provide a positive tailwind for SPY, while weakness may pressure the broader market given financials’ large index weighting.
narratives.next_week: Next week features a tech and industrial heavy slate with GOOGL, TSLA, HON, and RTX reporting around July 22-23. GOOGL’s ad revenue and AI investments will be key for Communication Services and growth sentiment, impacting NVDA given its AI exposure. Industrial earnings will offer insight into manufacturing demand and supply chain dynamics, which could influence cyclical sectors within SPY and consumer discretionary names like AMZN.
narratives.next_month: The following month sees a packed calendar with mega-cap tech earnings from MSFT, META, AAPL, and AMZN clustered around July 29-30, alongside the FOMC meeting and July PCE data. Market reaction to earnings and Fed guidance will be critical for growth stocks and overall risk sentiment, directly affecting SPY and tech bellwethers NVDA and AMZN. Inflation data will further shape rate expectations, influencing equity multiples and sector rotation into utilities and energy.
